concepts.title: Double Fertilization
concepts.description: Double fertilization is a unique process in angiosperms where one sperm fuses with the egg to form zygote and the other sperm fuses with two polar nuclei to form triploid primary endosperm nucleus.

concepts.title: Types of Pollination
concepts.description: Pollination can be classified into two types: Self-pollination (or self-fertilization) and Cross-pollination.

concepts.title: Role of Stigma
concepts.description: Stigma is the upper most part of the pistil and is responsible for receiving pollen grains. It also secretes enzymes to break down the pollen coat.

concepts.title: Ovules Structure
concepts.description: To remember the structure of ovules, use the mnemonic 'EMZY' which stands for Egg, Micropyle, Zygospor (nucellus), and Outer integument.

concepts.title: Confusion between Fertilization and Double Fertilization
concepts.description: Students often get confused between fertilization and double fertilization. Fertilization is the fusion of gametes to form zygote, while double fertilization is the fusion of two sperm cells with the egg and two polar nuclei.

concepts.title: Types of Fruits
concepts.description: Fruits can be classified into three types: Simple fruit, Aggregate fruit, and Composite fruit.

concepts.title: Parts of Stamen
concepts.description: The stamen consists of two main parts: Filament and Anther. The filament is the stalk that supports the anther, and the anther produces pollen grains.

concepts.title: Importance of Pollination
concepts.description: Pollination is essential for the reproduction of angiosperms as it leads to the production of fruits and seeds.
